bullets: Three studies in the contemporary AI era: two randomized field experiments reaching 1,337 and 366 phone customers, plus an online experiment with 238 participants on AI sales calls.
AI voice agents built on LLMs (model not stated) place outbound sales calls; performance is measured by conversion in field experiments, with no validation against any ground truth.
Disclosing the AI no longer lowers conversion versus nondisclosure, but AI agents still substantially underperform human agents on sales-process execution and empathy.
Administrative records from an online job posting platform plus a lower-information survey dataset, used to measure applicant to job match quality in hiring; period and geography not stated.
GPT simulates a human resources specialist assessing applicant suitability for a job; the resulting match measure is correlated with traditional measures, with no accuracy figure reported.
The LLM measure positively correlates with and adds information beyond traditional measures; revealing gender leads GPT to rate women better suited for female-dominated roles.
validation_note: correlated with traditional measures, no accuracy figure

bullets: Literature survey with no empirical sample, covering reported LLM applications across three business domains: finance, marketing, and supply chain management.
No model named; the paper reviews documented uses such as market prediction, fraud detection, personalized engagement, and demand forecasting rather than running any model.
Concludes that LLMs improve operational efficiency and strategic insight across the three domains and stresses synergistic cross-domain effects; no quantitative finding reported.
Conceptual econometric framework for empirical economics, with no specific dataset; organized around two uses, forecasting outcomes from text and estimating economic concepts for downstream analysis.
No model named; the framework requires no training leakage for prediction and pairing LLM outputs with a small validation sample for valid downstream estimation.
Without a validation sample, choices of model or prompt can produce dramatically different parameter estimates; the validation sample delivers consistent and precise estimates.
More than 16,000 Management Discussion and Analysis sections from US 10-K filings; unit of observation is the filing; sample period not stated.

An LLM (not named) scores four obfuscation dimensions, vagueness, hedging, positive spin, and inconsistency; checked for reproducibility and consistency with trained human judgments, with no agreement figure reported.
Measures are distinct from readability and tone indices, predict earnings management in expected directions, and differentially predict analyst forecast dispersion and accuracy.
validation_note: compared to human judgments, no agreement figure stated

bullets: Two forecasting tasks, news headlines predicting stock returns and earnings call transcripts predicting capital expenditures, over firm-date pairs spanning the LLM training cutoff.
No model named; a date-only recall query yields a Lookahead Propensity statistic estimating whether the LLM internalized realized outcomes, tested through an accuracy-regression interaction.
Forecast power is amplified on high-propensity firm-date pairs in sample and the interaction loses significance after the training cutoff, signaling lookahead-bias contamination.
A theoretical agent-based market populated by LLM-powered investors reacting to narrative information shocks, built to study stablecoin de-pegging risk; no real-world sample.
No model named; LLM agents process unstructured narrative data to form beliefs and trade, reproducing investor panic dynamics, with no validation against ground truth.
Crossing a narrative severity threshold triggers cognitive de-pegging, with expected maximum price deviation surging about 1,441 basis points as beliefs converge into synchronized selling.
35 widely used LLMs from five vendors, each queried with otherwise identical prompts that vary only one word, man versus woman, in a financial advice request.
Models not named individually; each is asked to recommend an equity allocation, and outputs are compared across the single-word gender change to detect differential advice.
Women are advised to allocate 1.7 percentage points less to equity funds; the gap persists across vendors and generations and attenuates but survives with richer risk information.
